items.title: How Texas Roadhouse Keeps Steak at $19 as Beef Prices Soar | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Texas Roadhouse is the largest casual dining chain by sales in the U.S. and has continued to keep their steak dinners affordable, even as beef prices have risen. Its free bread and peanuts continue to be a part of its core strategy to provide value and keep customers happy during periods of inflation

WSJ spoke with the restaurant’s CEO Jerry Morgan, who explains how he’s found a way to keep his operations lean and customers happy. 

Chapters: 
0:00 Texas Roadhouse’s sales
1:00 How the chain remains so successful 
1:33 Affording free peanuts and bread and cheap steaks
2:49 Providing peanuts in bags after Covid
3:40 Rising beef prices and controling costs
5:49 What’s next?

News Explainers
Some days the high-speed news cycle can bring more questions than answers. WSJ’s news explainers break down the day's biggest stories into bite-size pieces to help you make sense of the news.

#TexasRoadhouse #Food #WSJ</article>

items.title: China Cracked the 90-Second Drama. Now it's Taking Over American Phones. | WSJ
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>The Wall Street Journal gained behind-the-scenes access to the set of “Vicious,” a microdrama produced by ReelShort, the app at the center of a short-form video boom. These 90-second dramas draw millions of U.S. viewers, and ReelShort briefly rivaled TikTok for the top spot on the U.S. App Store. 

We explain how this bite-sized storytelling model is disrupting the American streaming landscape and challenging established platforms like Netflix. 

Chapters:
0:00 Microdramas, explained
0:46 How it started and why people watch
2:27 The economics of microdramas
5:09 The creative aspect
6:54 What’s next?

News Explainers
Some days the high-speed news cycle can bring more questions than answers. WSJ’s news explainers break down the day's biggest stories into bite-size pieces to help you make sense of the news.

#China #Film #WSJ</article>

items.title: Hillary Clinton and Rep. Nancy Mace Clash During Deposition
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>Hillary Clinton and Rep. Nancy Mace (R., S.C.) shouted at each other during the former secretary of state’s deposition before the House Oversight Committee.⁠
⁠
Hillary and Bill Clinton sat for depositions near their home in Chappaqua, N.Y., last week to answer questions about convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein and his associate, Ghislaine Maxwell.⁠

#Epstein #HillaryClinton #WSJ</article>

items.title: Iran's Deadly Protests: 'I Cannot Ever Go Back,' Says Witness
items.author: The Wall Street Journal
items.content:
<article>On Jan. 8, Iranians took to the streets to call for an end to the repressive Islamic Republic. Two days later, demonstrations had been largely stamped out, the country was in a communications blackout, and thousands of protesters had been killed.⁠
⁠
Four witnesses to the protests share firsthand testimony of how a popular uprising turned into scenes of terror. Their accounts, along with videos verified by The Wall Street Journal and analysis from weapons and ammunition experts, offer a chronology of 48 hours of violence

#Iran #MiddleEast #WSJ</article>
